translated: They cut their hair short so that their enemies and those who worshiped idols would not be able to grab them by their long hair during battle.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.

translated: In the past, we used to respect the elders, and we obeyed the elders. Every child used to fear adults, and young people used to fear adults very much.

translated: They said that I should tell you that, the reason they are giving you this money is so that you can buy things for yourself. But don't tell Asaala Dayua's children about it.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.

translated: If you want to do something good, 'Don't put off until tomorrow. Don't say I will do it tomorrow or the day after tomorrow, or say that I will do it when I have money, or when I have finished other work

translated: But when the young men saw that they were winning, they said, "Why should we die here for nothing? Come, let us flee," and they ran away leaving Asael David alone in the midst of the enemies.

text: Ti ba tubeha nɔtuurum ma'ara a nɛŋa, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra en, ti sɛba me lɔbera a pɛgɛhi,

translated: Why do we have to hide this thing? Tell the truth! Or do you want to keep lying to them until God comes?

translated: When evening came, they would bring to him all kinds of sick people. And many people who had heard about Jesus would say, "This person is very good. Let's take our sick ones."

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
